data_in.description: Minimal CUT&RUN bedGraph test file with chromosome, start, end, and signal columns

data_out.description: Peaked-regions output file from SEACR in BED or bedGraph format containing genomic coordinates and peak statistics
Verification log confirming cloned repository structure, script executability, and successful end-to-end run completion

domain_knowledge: CUT&RUN bedGraph format consists of four columns: chromosome, start coordinate, end coordinate, and signal intensity, and is the standard input for peak-calling tools like SEACR.

SEACR (Sparse Enrichment Analysis for CUT&RUN) is designed specifically for sparse epigenetic data from cleavage under targets and release under nuclease experiments and outputs peak regions in standard genomic coordinate formats.
Executable permissions on shell scripts (*.sh) and R scripts must be verified via file stat checks or chmod commands to ensure the cloned scripts can be invoked directly.
Peaked-regions output from SEACR contains chromosomal coordinates and associated peak statistics and should be validated for non-zero row counts and properly formatted BED-like structure.

direct_checks: verify repository can be cloned without errors
file_exists: shell script files (*.sh) in cloned repository root or standard subdirectories (bin/, scripts/)
file_exists: R script files (*.R) in cloned repository root or standard subdirectories (bin/, scripts/, R/)
verify shell scripts have executable permissions (mode includes +x)
verify R scripts are readable and syntactically valid R (script_runs on R parser without fatal errors)
script_runs: end-to-end SEACR execution with minimal valid CUT&RUN bedGraph input file
file_exists: peaked-regions output file produced after end-to-end run
file_format_is: output file has expected format (BED or similar tabular peak region format), robust to naming conventions

output_matches_reference: output structure contains expected columns for peak regions (chromosome, start, end, score or similar), no canonical answer for exact column names or order across SEACR versions
expert_review: confirm that the cloned SEACR repository is the correct and current version intended for CUT&RUN analysis
assess whether the minimal test bedGraph input is representative of real CUT&RUN data and appropriate for validating end-to-end functionality
evaluate whether the peaked-regions output file content is biologically sensible (e.g., peak coordinates fall within expected genomic ranges, score values are in expected ranges)

cmd: set -e && git clone https://github.com/FredHutch/SEACR.git seacr_repo && cd seacr_repo && ls -la && file SEACR.sh && chmod +x SEACR.sh && test -f SEACR.R && Rscript SEACR.R --help 2>&1 | head -5 || true && bash SEACR.sh {inputs.bedgraph_input} 1.0 non peaks.bed && test -s peaks.bed && head peaks.bed && echo 'SEACR reconstruction and validation complete' > ../validation.log

workflow_description: 1. Clone the FredHutch/SEACR repository from GitHub using git. 2. Verify that shell scripts and R scripts are present in the cloned repository and have executable permissions set. 3. Prepare a minimal CUT&RUN bedGraph input file meeting SEACR format requirements. 4. Execute SEACR end-to-end on the test bedGraph input using the cloned scripts. 5. Verify that the peaked-regions output file is produced and contains valid region coordinates.
